conflicts.description: SAP inventory sync is required to be real-time AND batch (once daily) simultaneously — two incompatible synchronization strategies for the same data source.

version_a.text: Inventory levels must sync with SAP in real time to prevent overselling.

version_b.text: Product information must sync from SAP via batch process once daily.

conflicts.description: Vendors are stated to manage their own product listings, but a separate business rule explicitly prohibits vendors from setting prices — creating ambiguity about the scope of vendor listing management and conflicting with the implied capability of full listing control.

version_a.text: Vendors must be able to manage their product listings and inventory via portal.

version_b.text: Vendors cannot set product prices; only RetailCorp central admin controls pricing.

conflicts.description: Vendors are described as managing their own product listings, but the central admin is also assigned the ability to add, edit, and deactivate products — creating overlapping and potentially conflicting ownership of product catalog management.

version_a.text: Vendor partners manage their own inventory and product listings via dedicated login.

version_b.text: The system shall allow central admin to add, edit, and deactivate products in the catalog.

conflicts.description: GDPR compliance is required due to NRI customer base, but GDPR is a European regulation that conflicts with the Indian IT Act compliance requirement — the two regulatory frameworks have differing and sometimes incompatible obligations (e.g., data subject rights, breach notification timelines, consent mechanisms).

version_a.text: Platform must comply with GDPR regulations due to NRI customer base.

version_b.text: Platform must comply with Indian IT Act regulations.

conflicts.description: Real-time inventory sync with SAP is required to prevent overselling, but product information syncs only once daily via batch — if inventory is part of product information, these two requirements directly contradict each other on sync frequency.

version_a.text: Inventory levels must sync with SAP in real time to prevent overselling.

version_b.text: Product information must sync from SAP via batch process once daily.

conflicts.description: US-015 requires real-time inventory sync with SAP, while TR-028 mandates a once-daily batch sync for product data from SAP — these are incompatible synchronization models for overlapping SAP data.

version_a.text: The system shall sync inventory levels with SAP in real time to reflect accurate stock status.

version_b.text: Product information must sync from SAP via batch process once daily.

conflicts.description: Central admin controls pricing per TR-021 and US-014, but TR-008 and TR-020 grant vendors management of their own product listings — if listings include pricing fields, vendor portal access conflicts with the admin-only pricing rule.

version_a.text: The system shall allow central admin to set and update product prices and discounts.

version_b.text: The system shall allow vendors to manage their own product listings on the platform.

conflicts.description: ZestMoney partnership may not be finalized before development begins, yet no corresponding functional requirement excludes ZestMoney — if development proceeds assuming ZestMoney integration, the assumption of an unfinalized agreement conflicts with building dependent functionality within the 6-month constraint.

version_a.text: ZestMoney partnership agreement may not be finalized before development begins.

version_b.text: Platform must go live within 6 months maximum from project start.

gaps.message: 'Business Context and Background' has limited coverage.
'Project Objectives and Success Criteria' has limited coverage.
